title: Decomposing 3/4 as a Sum of Unit Fractions

steps.explanation: Any fraction a/b can be thought of as 'a' copies of the unit fraction 1/b.

steps.instruction: Write 3 copies of 1/4 as an addition expression.
steps.explanation: Since 3/4 is three fourths, we add 1/4 three times.
When adding fractions with the same denominator, add the numerators and keep the denominator.

steps.instruction: 7/3 is greater than 1 because 7 > 3. How many wholes are inside?
steps.explanation: When the numerator is larger than the denominator, the fraction contains one or more wholes.

steps.instruction: Take out as many groups of 3/3 as possible.
steps.explanation: We can remove two groups of 3/3 (two wholes), leaving 1/3.

steps.instruction: Combine the whole number and the leftover fraction.
steps.explanation: The decomposition shows that 7/3 equals 2 whole and 1/3 more.
